Inventory Snap ID - PK value for this table.
Item Number
Item Client ID
Generation Date: Date and time the inventory snapshot was generated.
Received Quantity: Number of units received between the last time a snapshot was generated and the Generation Date.
Shipped Quantity: Number of units shipped between the last time a snapshot was generated and the Generation Date.

No of units adjusted between the last time a snapshot was generated and Generation Date. An adjustment includes adding, modifying or deleting inventory through invadjopr and cntaudopr, as well as inventory moved to WIP supply areas.
Work Order Consumed Quantity: Number of units delivered to a work order processing location between the last time a snapshot was generated and the Generation Date.

Number of units received through a WO between the last time a snapshot was generated and Generation Date. This includes inventory received from a WO either through production of a top level item or the return to stock of components.

The results here will be dictated by a policy. It will either be: the qty of this item number/item client id within four-wall locations or  specified inventory status progression and is within four-wall locations.
Shippable Quantity: A subset of the On-Hand Inventory Quantity that only includes on-hand inventory with an inventory status that is shippable.
Ordered Quantity: The quantity of the item required for outstanding order lines.
Shipment Planned Quantity: The quantity of the item number/item client id that has already been assigned to a shipment that is not closed or cancelled.
The total qty of this item number/item client id that is expected, but has not yet been putaway and received into inventory. The results here will be dictated by a policy
Warehouse ID

Uniquely identifies the storage location.
Warehouse ID - The warehouse where the aisle is located.
Defines the area to which this location belongs.
Specifies if locations are tracked at a unit quantity (Q), volume(V), length (L), case dimension(C) or pallet footprint(P) level.
Current disposition of the location.  One of:  Full (F), Partial (P), Empty (E) or Inventory Error (I).
The velocity zone of the location.
Aisle ID - A collection of locations in a row.
The relative distance between locations, the travel sequence is during work management to find the closest location to an idle user.
Storage Sequence is used to influence how locations are ordered when they are considered for inbound inventory during Putaway.

Used to path inventory through processing locations.  The resource code is a mechanism by which that location is reserved for product matching the rescod.  For example, in a shipment staging lane, the rescod is often set to the carcod.
The bay the location belongs to.
The level in the rack the location is on.
The position of the location respective to it's level.

This represents the hgt (in the same units as used on the ftpmst table) of product that the loc will tolerate.  This is evaluated on a per storage request.  For example, in a pallet racking example, the lochgt may be set to 90 (inches).
This field is currently NOT used for storage selection or volume calculation purposes and is only used during 3PL Billing operations.
This field is currently NOT used for storage selection or volume calculation purposes and is only used during 3PL Billing operations.
Dock access code.

A 'checksum' or alternate identifier which may be used to identify the location.  This is often used in facilities where locations may not be able to be labeled and it is desired to provide an alternate, easy to type, name for the loc.
The loccod will indicate what this number actually means (in terms of volume, pallets, etc...).  This represents the maximum storage capacity of the location.
Much the same as maxqvl, this field is used to indicate the currently used capacity in a location.
Much the same as the other 'qvl' numbers, this field is used to indicate how much capacity is pending storage to the location.

Indicates what percentage of the maxqvl should be used when performing top-off replenishments.  A value of 0 will indicate 100%.  50 will indicate half full.  This can be useful if it is desired to 'overfill' a loc during repl process
Much the same as trfpct, this field indicates the percentage of maxqvl which should be used during emergency replenishment operations.
Escalation Type - Location capacity escalation type.
Escalation Threshold - Escalation Threshold
This flag indicates that the location is usable.  If set to false, future allocations and stores are disabled at this location.
This flag indicates that the location is available for storage operations.
This flag indicates that the location is available for picking (allocation) operations.
This flag indicates that the location may be replenished.

This flag indicates that this loc is permanently assigned. In processing area, it applies to rescod value that is set. In normal sto. areas, it indicates itmnum is assigned to loc. STORE-ASG-LOC policies tells the item that is assigned
This flag indicates that there is a count in-progress at this location.
Much like travel sequence, this field is used to sequence counting activities.
Operational column used to track the number of counts performed during a particular cycle.  Used when location based cycle counting is in effect.
Used to schedule location based cycle counts much in the same was as the itmmst.abccod is used.
Date location was last counted.
Label device which is closest to this location.

Used to indicate that this loc is locked for processing.  Often used by operational programs such as order overpack operations to indicate that a work station is processing a particular lane - this prevents collisions during processing.

Used to indicate that special vehicle access is necessary to get to this location.  This is used by the work manangement system to ensure that work is only sent to devices if the device can access the location.
Voice Check Digit - This will be a short code that will be used in voice to identify a location.

Voice Check Digit 3 - This will be a short code that will be used in voice to identify a location in addition to the standard Check Digit, used when the check digit rotation policy is configured.
